q: Spectacles are optical instruments that employ lenses.
In cameras, the image can only be recorded on films and not digitally.
The camera uses a concave lens to form an image.
The image formed on the film of a camera is real and inverted.
The image formed on the film of a camera is enlarged.
The diaphragm in a camera is a circular opening that controls the amount of light.
The smaller the f-number, the narrower the aperture is.
When taking a photograph in bright light, a small aperture must be used.
A shutter is used in a camera to control the time the light falls on the film.
A simple microscope consists of a single converging lens of short focal length.
The magnifying power of a simple microscope is always less than 1.
In a simple microscope, the object is placed between the focus and the optical center.
The image formed by a simple microscope is virtual, erect, and magnified.
A compound microscope consists of two converging lenses.
The lens near the object in a compound microscope is called the eyepiece.
The focal length of the objective lens in a compound microscope is very large.
The objective of a compound microscope forms a real, inverted, and magnified image.
The final image formed by a compound microscope is virtual and inverted with respect to the object.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is the product of the magnification of the objective and the magnifying power of the eyepiece.
The astronomical telescope is used to view objects on the Earth's surface.
An astronomical telescope consists of two converging lenses.
In an astronomical telescope, the objective lens has a larger focal length than the eyepiece.
The final image in an astronomical telescope is virtual and erect.
In normal adjustment of a telescope, the final image is formed at infinity.
The length of an astronomical telescope in normal adjustment is fo + fe.
A terrestrial telescope produces a final image that is erect with respect to the object.
A terrestrial telescope uses an erecting lens between the objective and the eyepiece.
The erecting lens in a terrestrial telescope increases the magnifying power.
The Galilean telescope uses a diverging lens as the eyepiece.
The final image in a Galilean telescope is virtual and inverted.
The length of a Galilean telescope is fo - fe.
The eye is very similar to a camera in its optical function.
The crystalline lens of the eye is a converging lens.
The retina acts as a screen on which the image is formed.
Accommodation is the process by which the eye changes its focal length.
The near point for a normal human eye is approximately 25 cm.
Myopia is a defect where the eye cannot see distant objects clearly.
Myopia is corrected using a convex lens.
Hypermetropia is a defect where the eye cannot see nearby objects clearly.
Hypermetropia is corrected using a converging lens.
In a myopic eye, the image of a distant object is formed behind the retina.
Presbyopia is a defect that usually occurs with old age.
The power of a lens is measured in diopters.
The power of a converging lens is negative.
The magnification of a lens is the ratio of image height to object height.
Refracting telescopes use mirrors to gather light.
The objective of a camera is usually a single simple lens.
A prism binocular uses total internal reflection to fold the light path.
The resolving power of a telescope increases with the diameter of the objective.
Chromatice aberration occurs in lenses but not in mirrors.
